findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: In the PDF slide 2 table, the 11/14/2013 row spans across the Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns as a single merged cell, but in the HTML conversion, the content is split into separate cells for Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns, each repeating the text.
findings.evidence: PDF: 11/14/2013 row has merged content across Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns as one cell. HTML: The same text appears duplicated in both the Jordan Lake Water Supply Allocations column and the Cape Fear River Basin Water Supply Plan column separately.

findings.description: In the PDF slide 2 table, the 9/11/2014 row spans across the Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns as a single merged cell, but in the HTML conversion, the content is split into separate cells with duplicated text.
findings.evidence: PDF: 9/11/2014 row has 'EMC decision at the September 2014 meeting.' spanning across columns as merged. HTML: The text is duplicated in both Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns separately.

findings.description: In the PDF slide 2 table, the EMC Model Approval entries for dates 1/1/2013 through 5/1/2013 appear to span multiple rows in the Cape Fear-Neuse column, but in the HTML they are repeated in each individual row.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'EMC Model Approval' appears once spanning rows from 1/1/2013 to 5/1/2013 in the Hydrologic Model column. HTML: 'EMC Model Approval' is repeated individually in rows for 1/1/2013, 2/1/2013, 4/1/2013, and 5/1/2013.

findings.description: In the schedule table, the 'EMC Model Approval' entry in the Cape Fear – Neuse River Basin Hydrologic Model column is duplicated across rows for 1/1/2013, 2/1/2013, 4/1/2013, and 5/1/2013. In the PDF, 'EMC Model Approval' appears to span rows 1/1/2013 through 2/1/2013 (or possibly further), but the HTML repeats it in four separate rows instead of using a rowspan or leaving cells empty where appropriate.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'EMC Model Approval' listed once spanning the 2/1/2013 row area. HTML duplicates it in rows for 1/1/2013, 2/1/2013, 4/1/2013, and 5/1/2013.

findings.description: In the schedule table, the row for 11/14/2013 in the PDF shows the text spanning across the Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns as a single merged entry. The HTML duplicates the text into both the Jordan Lake Water Supply Allocations column and the Cape Fear River Basin Water Supply Plan column separately, and similarly for 9/11/2014.
findings.evidence: PDF 11/14/2013 row: 'Final Water Supply Plan and DWR's Allocation Recommendations presented at the EMC's November 2013 meeting.' spans across columns. HTML duplicates this text in both columns separately. Same issue for 9/11/2014 row.

findings.description: The footer text 'Managing Water Resources to Support North Carolina's Future' appears as a heading at the top of the HTML document body before the Agenda section, duplicating the footer content from the PDF as a standalone element.
findings.evidence: PDF has 'Managing Water Resources to Support North Carolina's Future' as a footer/tagline at bottom of slides. HTML places it before the main content as well as the sections containing it are separated.
